title: Tui sees summer sales fall 10% due to cautious UK customers
snippet: The travel operator says customers are delaying booking holidays over Iran war concerns.

title: Trade, Iran and Taiwan on the agenda as Trump arrives in China for high-stakes talks with Xi
snippet: The US president says his first request to Xi would be to  "open up" China, while Beijing is expected to press Trump on the status of Taiwan.

title: MPs call for sunbed advertising ban to help prevent skin cancer
snippet: A report by a cross-party group of MPs has found the majority of skin cancer cases are preventable.

title: Daily pill to help keep weight off after stopping obesity jabs
snippet: The tablet - orforglipron - is available in the US and could soon launch in the UK.

title: Shrinking Milka chocolate bar tricked consumers, says German court
snippet: A court in Bremen has found the manufacturer of the classic Alpine Milk chocolate bar guilty of "shrinkflation".

title: Court overturns Alex Murdaugh's murder convictions and orders new trial
snippet: The court has ordered a new trial over the June 2021 killings of Paul and Maggie Murdaugh.

title: Massive Alaska megatsunami was second largest ever recorded
snippet: New research suggests glacier melt driven by climate change is increasing the risk of giant waves.

title: Posh sandwich has more salt than nearly five cheeseburgers
snippet: Action on Salt & Sugar said people should not be exposed to a "hidden health risk every time they buy lunch".

title: WhatsApp launches totally private 'incognito' conversations with its AI chatbot
snippet: A cyber security expert says deleting chat history could lead to a lack of accountability if things go wrong.

title: Suicide forum fined £950,000 for not blocking UK users
snippet: The regulator said the forum had not done enough to protect UK users - but critics accuse Ofcom of acting too slowly.

title: WhatsApp launches totally private 'incognito' conversations with its AI chatbot
snippet: A cyber security expert says deleting chat history could lead to a lack of accountability if things go wrong.

title: Smart glasses are 'an invasion of privacy' - Meta's are selling better than ever
snippet: The biggest tech firms are set to sell millions of smart glasses despite growing privacy concerns.

title: Global forest loss slows but El Niño fires could threaten progress
snippet: The loss of tropical rainforests eased last year, according to new analysis, but scientists warn they are still disappearing rapidly.

title: White-tailed eagles to be released in Exmoor despite farmer warnings
snippet: Some farmers fear the reintroduction of the UK's biggest bird of prey will threaten their livestock.

title: Dozens of dogs rescued and suspect arrested in Uganda after BBC investigation
snippet: A BBC investigation exposed how scammers posted social content of dogs in distress to dupe donors into giving money.
